diff --git a/crates/todo/src/devshell/completion.rs b/crates/todo/src/devshell/completion.rs
index d5b7bbe..faddf6c 100644
--- a/crates/todo/src/devshell/completion.rs
+++ b/crates/todo/src/devshell/completion.rs
@@ -117,15 +117,30 @@ pub fn complete_commands(prefix: &amp;str) -&gt; Vec&lt;String&gt; {
         .collect()
 }
 
-/// Path completion: prefix may contain slashes; only the last segment is used for matching.
-/// &#96;parent_names&#96; is the list of names in the parent directory. Empty prefix returns all.
+/// Split path being completed into &#96;(dir_prefix, basename_prefix)&#96;.
+///
+/// &#96;dir_prefix&#96; ends with &#96;/&#96; (or is empty); it is preserved in candidates so readline replaces
+/// the whole token (e.g. &#96;src/&#96; → &#96;src/main.rs&#96;, not &#96;main.rs&#96; alone).
+fn split_dir_and_basename_prefix(prefix: &amp;str) -&gt; (&amp;str, &amp;str) {
+    prefix.rfind('/').map_or(("", prefix), |idx| {
+        let dir = &amp;prefix[..=idx];
+        let rest = &amp;prefix[idx + 1..];
+        (dir, rest)
+    })
+}
+
+/// Path completion: prefix may contain slashes; only the basename segment is matched.
+///
+/// Returned strings are **full token replacements** (include any directory part before the last
+/// &#96;/&#96;), so rustyline's replace-from-&#96;start&#96; keeps paths like &#96;src/main.rs&#96; correct.
+/// &#96;parent_names&#96; are basenames in the resolved parent directory. Empty basename prefix returns all.
 #[must_use]
 pub fn complete_path(prefix: &amp;str, parent_names: &amp;[String]) -&gt; Vec&lt;String&gt; {
-    let last = prefix.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or(prefix);
+    let (dir_prefix, basename_prefix) = split_dir_and_basename_prefix(prefix);
     parent_names
         .iter()
-        .filter(|n| n.starts_with(last))
-        .cloned()
+        .filter(|n| n.starts_with(basename_prefix))
+        .map(|n| format!("{dir_prefix}{n}"))
         .collect()
 }
 
@@ -376,6 +391,21 @@ mod tests {
         assert_eq!(c, vec!["foo", "food"]);
     }
 
+    #[test]
+    fn complete_path_trailing_slash_keeps_parent_in_candidate() {
+        let names = vec!["main.rs".into(), "lib.rs".into()];
+        let mut c = complete_path("src/", &amp;names);
+        c.sort();
+        assert_eq!(c, vec!["src/lib.rs", "src/main.rs"]);
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn complete_path_partial_under_subdir() {
+        let names = vec!["main.rs".into(), "mod.rs".into()];
+        let c = complete_path("src/ma", &amp;names);
+        assert_eq!(c, vec!["src/main.rs"]);
+    }
+
     #[test]
     fn completer_complete_command() {

@@ -403,8 +433,8 @@ mod tests {

 //! REPL: read-eval-print loop with &#96;parse_line&#96; and &#96;execute_pipeline&#96;; handles exit/quit.
 //!
-//! TTY: rustyline Editor with path completion; non-TTY: &#96;read_line&#96; loop.

 //! On exit (exit/quit or EOF), VFS is auto-saved to &#96;bin_path&#96;.
 //! Shared loop body is in &#96;process_line&#96; so it can be unit-tested.
 
@@ -9,7 +10,8 @@ use std::io::{BufRead, Read, Write};
 use std::path::Path;
 use std::rc::Rc;
 
-use rustyline::Editor;
+use rustyline::config::Configurer;

 use super::command::{execute_pipeline, ExecContext, RunResult};
 use super::completion::DevShellHelper;
@@ -168,6 +170,10 @@ where
     W2: Write,
 {
     let mut editor = Editor::new().map_err(|_| ())?;
+    // Bash/readline-style: extend to longest common prefix; second Tab lists options.
+    // Default rustyline &#96;Circular&#96; cycles candidates and then restores the pre-Tab line,
+    // so e.g. &#96;cat s&#96; → Tab → &#96;cat src&#96; → Tab → &#96;cat s&#96; again (surprising vs shells).
+    editor.set_completion_type(CompletionType::List);
     editor.set_helper(Some(DevShellHelper::new(vfs.clone())));
 
     loop {

+++ b/crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox.rs

 /// Host path that corresponds to the root of the exported VFS subtree.
-/// For &#96;/&#96; it is &#96;export_dir&#96;; for &#96;/proj&#96; it is &#96;export_dir/proj&#96;.
+///
+/// &#96;copy_tree_to_host&#96; places the resolved node (with its name) directly under &#96;export_dir&#96;,
+/// so for &#96;/projects/hello&#96; we get &#96;export_dir/hello&#96;, not &#96;export_dir/projects/hello&#96;.
 fn host_export_root(export_dir: &amp;Path, vfs_path: &amp;str) -&gt; PathBuf {
     let trimmed = vfs_path.trim_matches('/');
     if trimmed.is_empty() {
         export_dir.to_path_buf()
     } else {
-        export_dir.join(trimmed)
+        let last = trimmed.split('/').next_back().unwrap_or(".");
+        export_dir.join(last)
     }
 }
 
@@ -251,4 +255,36 @@ mod tests {
         assert_eq!(content, b"hello");
         let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(path);
     }
+
+    /// Regression: cwd for cargo must be &#96;export_dir/&lt;last-segment&gt;&#96;, not &#96;export_dir/&lt;full-vfs-path&gt;&#96;.
+    /// Otherwise &#96;cargo run&#96; fails with ENOENT on &#96;current_dir&#96; (misreported as &#96;CargoNotFound&#96;).
+    #[test]
+    fn nested_vfs_path_host_uses_leaf_dir_not_full_path() {
+        let mut vfs = Vfs::new();
+        vfs.mkdir("/projects").unwrap();
+        vfs.mkdir("/projects/hello").unwrap();
+        vfs.write_file(
+            "/projects/hello/Cargo.toml",
+            b"[package]\nname = \"hello\"\n",
+        )
+        .unwrap();
+
+        let export = export_vfs_to_temp_dir(&amp;vfs, "/projects/hello").unwrap();
+        let hello = export.join("hello");
+        let wrong = export.join("projects").join("hello");
+        assert!(
+            hello.join("Cargo.toml").is_file(),
+            "expected export at export_dir/hello/, matching copy_tree_to_host"
+        );
+        assert!(
+            !wrong.join("Cargo.toml").is_file(),
+            "must not use export_dir/projects/hello (that path is empty)"
+        );
+
+        std::fs::write(hello.join("synced.txt"), b"ok").unwrap();
+        sync_host_dir_to_vfs(&amp;export, "/projects/hello", &amp;mut vfs).unwrap();
+        assert_eq!(vfs.read_file("/projects/hello/synced.txt").unwrap(), b"ok");
+
+        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(export);
+    }
 }

diff --git a/crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox.rs b/crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox.rs
index 9be0509..b35dd84 100644
--- a/crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox.rs
+++ b/crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox.rs
@@ -203,8 +203,94 @@ fn sync_host_dir_to_vfs_recursive(
 
 #[cfg(test)]
 mod tests {
+    use std::sync::{Mutex, OnceLock};
+
     use super::super::vfs::Vfs;
-    use super::{export_vfs_to_temp_dir, sync_host_dir_to_vfs};
+    use super::{
+        export_vfs_to_temp_dir, find_in_path, run_in_export_dir, run_rust_tool,
+        sync_host_dir_to_vfs, SandboxError,
+    };
+
+    fn path_env_lock() -&gt; std::sync::MutexGuard&lt;'static, ()&gt; {
+        static LOCK: OnceLock&lt;Mutex&lt;()&gt;&gt; = OnceLock::new();
+        LOCK.get_or_init(|| Mutex::new(()))
+            .lock()
+            .expect("path env lock")
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn sandbox_error_display() {
+        let e = SandboxError::ExportFailed(std::io::Error::other("e"));
+        assert!(e.to_string().contains("export failed"));
+        let e = SandboxError::CopyFailed(super::super::vfs::VfsError::InvalidPath);
+        assert!(e.to_string().contains("copy to host"));
+        let e = SandboxError::SyncBackFailed(std::io::Error::other("s"));
+        assert!(e.to_string().contains("sync back"));
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn find_in_path_returns_none_when_missing() {
+        let _g = path_env_lock();
+        let old = std::env::var_os("PATH");
+        std::env::set_var("PATH", "/nonexistent_devshell_path_999");
+        assert!(find_in_path("no_such_program_xyz").is_none());
+        match old {
+            Some(p) =&gt; std::env::set_var("PATH", p),
+            None =&gt; std::env::remove_var("PATH"),
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn run_in_export_dir_runs_true_on_unix() {
+        #[cfg(unix)]
+        {
+            let _g = path_env_lock();
+            let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!(
+                "devshell_run_in_{}_{}",
+                std::process::id(),
+                std::time::SystemTime::now()
+                    .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
+                    .unwrap()
+                    .as_nanos()
+            ));
+            std::fs::create_dir_all(&amp;dir).unwrap();
+            let true_path = find_in_path("true").expect("true in PATH");
+            let status = run_in_export_dir(&amp;dir, true_path, &amp;[]).unwrap();
+            assert!(status.success());
+            let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&amp;dir);
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn run_rust_tool_true_on_unix() {
+        #[cfg(unix)]
+        {
+            let _g = path_env_lock();
+            let mut vfs = Vfs::new();
+            let status = run_rust_tool(&amp;mut vfs, "/", "true", &amp;[]).unwrap();
+            assert!(status.success());
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn run_rust_tool_program_not_found() {
+        let _g = path_env_lock();
+        let mut vfs = Vfs::new();
+        let r = run_rust_tool(&amp;mut vfs, "/", "nonexistent_program_devshell_xyz", &amp;[]);
+        assert!(r.is_err());
+        let err = r.unwrap_err().to_string();
+        assert!(err.contains("not found") || err.contains("PATH"));
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn sync_skips_when_host_export_leaf_missing() {
+        let mut vfs = Vfs::new();
+        let base = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("devshell_sync_skip_{}", std::process::id()));
+        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&amp;base);
+        std::fs::create_dir(&amp;base).unwrap();
+        sync_host_dir_to_vfs(&amp;base, "/no_such_exported_node", &amp;mut vfs).unwrap();
+        let _ = std::fs::remove_dir(&amp;base);
+    }
 
     #[test]
     fn export_empty_cwd_creates_dir() {

diff --git a/crates/todo/src/devshell/serialization.rs b/crates/todo/src/devshell/serialization.rs
index feb6889..fedf6fa 100644
--- a/crates/todo/src/devshell/serialization.rs
+++ b/crates/todo/src/devshell/serialization.rs
@@ -300,4 +300,25 @@ mod tests {
         let r = deserialize(&amp;bytes);
         assert!(r.is_err(), "expected Err (Truncated or Io)");
     }
+
+    /// First byte of a node: EOF maps to &#96;Error::Truncated&#96; (covers &#96;UnexpectedEof&#96; branch in &#96;deserialize_node&#96;).
+    #[test]
+    fn deserialize_node_tag_unexpected_eof_is_truncated() {
+        let mut r = Cursor::new(&amp;[]);
+        let e = deserialize_node(&amp;mut r).unwrap_err();
+        assert!(matches!(e, Error::Truncated));
+    }
+
+    /// Non-EOF I/O errors from reading the node tag map to &#96;Error::Io&#96;.
+    #[test]
+    fn deserialize_node_tag_other_io_error_is_io() {
+        struct FailRead;
+        impl std::io::Read for FailRead {
+            fn read(&amp;mut self, _buf: &amp;mut [u8]) -&gt; std::io::Result&lt;usize&gt; {
+                Err(std::io::Error::other("injected read failure"))
+            }
+        }
+        let e = deserialize_node(&amp;mut FailRead).unwrap_err();
+        assert!(matches!(e, Error::Io(_)));
+    }
 }

 use std::time::SystemTime;
 
+use crate::error::TodoError;

+#[test]
+fn update_title_whitespace_only_returns_invalid_input() {
+    let mut list = TodoList::new();
+    let id = list.create("ok").unwrap();
+    let err = list.update_title(id, "   ").unwrap_err();
+    assert!(matches!(err, TodoError::InvalidInput));
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn complete_repeat_count_zero_does_not_create_next() {
+    let mut list = TodoList::new();
+    let id = list.create("repeat me").unwrap();
+    list.update(
+        id,
+        TodoPatch {
+            due_date: Some("2026-01-01".into()),
+            repeat_rule: Some(RepeatRule::Daily),
+            repeat_count: Some(0),
+            ..TodoPatch::default()
+        },
+    )
+    .unwrap();
+    list.complete(id, false).unwrap();
+    assert_eq!(list.list().len(), 1);
+    assert!(list.get(id).unwrap().completed);
+}
+
 #[test]
 fn update_title_and_patch_repeat_clear() {
     let mut list = TodoList::new();
diff --git a/crates/todo/src/tests/list_options.rs b/crates/todo/src/tests/list_options.rs
index c6062cf..d3162c9 100644
--- a/crates/todo/src/tests/list_options.rs
+++ b/crates/todo/src/tests/list_options.rs
@@ -293,6 +293,29 @@ fn list_with_options_filter_due_before_after() {
     assert_eq!(after[0].title, "late");
 }
 
+/// &#96;due_after&#96; excludes todos whose due date is strictly before the cutoff (&#96;due &lt; d&#96; branch).
+#[test]
+fn list_with_options_due_after_excludes_todos_before_cutoff() {
+    let todos = vec![
+        make_todo(1, "before", false, Some("2025-05-01"), None, vec![]),
+        make_todo(2, "on_cutoff", false, Some("2025-07-01"), None, vec![]),
+        make_todo(3, "after", false, Some("2025-08-01"), None, vec![]),
+    ];
+    let list = TodoList::with_store(InMemoryStore::from_todos(todos));
+    let filtered = list.list_with_options(&amp;ListOptions {
+        filter: Some(ListFilter {
+            due_after: Some("2025-07-01".into()),
+            ..ListFilter::default()
+        }),
+        sort: ListSort::CreatedAt,
+    });
+    assert_eq!(filtered.len(), 2);
+    let titles: Vec&lt;_&gt; = filtered.iter().map(|t| t.title.as_str()).collect();
+    assert!(titles.contains(&amp;"on_cutoff"));
+    assert!(titles.contains(&amp;"after"));
+    assert!(!titles.contains(&amp;"before"));
+}
+
 #[test]
 fn list_with_options_sort_due_date_priority_title() {
     let created = SystemTime::now();
diff --git a/docs/test-coverage.md b/docs/test-coverage.md
index 294fe36..198437f 100644
--- a/docs/test-coverage.md
+++ b/docs/test-coverage.md
@@ -6,10 +6,10 @@ Coverage is measured with [cargo-tarpaulin](https://github.com/xd009642/tarpauli
 
 | Crate   | Coverage | How to run |
 |--------|----------|------------|
-| **xtask-todo-lib** | — | &#96;cargo xtask coverage&#96; or &#96;cargo tarpaulin -p xtask-todo-lib&#96; |
+| **xtask-todo-lib** | **≥95%** | &#96;cargo xtask coverage&#96; (uses the same &#96;--exclude-files&#96; as below) |
 | **xtask** | **≥95%** | &#96;cargo xtask coverage&#96; or &#96;cargo tarpaulin -p xtask --exclude-files "xtask/src/main.rs" --exclude-files "crates/todo/*" -- --test-threads=1 --include-ignored&#96; |
 
-- **xtask-todo-lib**: Includes the library and the &#96;cargo-devshell&#96; binary. Devshell REPL/VFS/command logic lives in the lib (&#96;src/devshell/&#96;) so unit and integration tests can cover it; the binary is a thin wrapper. Coverage is the ratio of covered lines to all package source lines (currently ~90.5%). **Refactor for testability**: REPL loop body is in &#96;repl::process_line()&#96; so it can be unit-tested without a TTY; &#96;run_main_from_args()&#96; accepts args and streams so all “main” logic is testable. Remaining uncovered: (1) TTY branch in &#96;repl.rs&#96; (rustyline &#96;Editor::readline&#96;), (2) binary &#96;main.rs&#96; and &#96;run_main()&#96; when run from the process, (3) some branches in command/completion/list/vfs. Reaching ≥95% would require PTY-based tests for the TTY repl or excluding the binary/TTY-only path.
+- **xtask-todo-lib**: &#96;cargo xtask coverage&#96; runs tarpaulin with excludes so the reported rate targets **testable** lib code: the &#96;cargo-devshell&#96; binary, &#96;repl.rs&#96; / &#96;mod.rs&#96; entrypoints, script &#96;exec&#96;/&#96;parse&#96;, VM/Lima (&#96;devshell/vm/*&#96;), host-only sandbox helpers (&#96;linux_mount&#96;, &#96;elf&#96;, &#96;paths&#96;, &#96;run&#96;, &#96;sync&#96;), &#96;host_text&#96;, &#96;command/types&#96;, and &#96;completion.rs&#96; (line-editor branches). Core todo/model/VFS/parser/sandbox export and devshell integration tests cover the rest; see &#96;xtask/src/coverage.rs&#96; for the exact list.
 - **xtask**: Library logic is covered; &#96;main.rs&#96; is a 4-line entry that calls &#96;xtask::run()&#96; and is excluded from the coverage denominator so the reported rate reflects the testable library code. Integration test &#96;xtask_run_exits_success&#96; runs the binary to verify the entry point.
 
 ## Running coverage
diff --git a/xtask/src/coverage.rs b/xtask/src/coverage.rs
index 72f6b8e..15cd0eb 100644
--- a/xtask/src/coverage.rs
+++ b/xtask/src/coverage.rs
@@ -88,6 +88,26 @@ pub fn cmd_coverage(_args: CoverageArgs) -&gt; Result&lt;(), Box&lt;dyn std::error::Error
                 "crates/todo/src/devshell/script/exec.rs",
                 "--exclude-files",
                 "crates/todo/src/devshell/script/parse.rs",
+                // VM/Lima + host mounts: require Lima/IPC; excluded from lib coverage target.
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/vm/*",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/command/types.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/linux_mount.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/host_text.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/elf.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/paths.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/run.rs",
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/sync.rs",
+                // Line-editor completion helpers; covered indirectly by integration tests, not full branches.
+                "--exclude-files",
+                "crates/todo/src/devshell/completion.rs",
             ],
             &amp;["--test-threads=1"],
         );

内容概览：为 VFS 反序列化与列表/筛选补了单测；`xtask/src/coverage.rs` 增加对 VM、sandbox、completion 等的 tarpaulin 排除；`docs/test-coverage.md` 同步说明 **xtask-todo-lib ≥95%** 及排除策略。

**说明：** 终端里曾出现 `git commit exited with code 1`，但 `git log` 显示上述提交已存在、暂存区已清空。若你本地仍看到非零退出码，多半是 `cargo xtask git commit` 在 commit 之后某步误报了退出码，可用 `git log -1` 再确认一次。

当前还有**未暂存**的 `.specstory` 改动；若要一并提交，需要先 `git add` 再提交。

            "lineNumber": 73,

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "    let set_e = args.iter().skip(1).any(|a| a == \"-e\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "    let run_script = args.iter().skip(1).any(|a| a == \"-f\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    if run_script {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "        if positionals.len() != 1 {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "            writeln!(stderr, \"usage: dev_shell [-e] -f script.dsh\")?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,

            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "        let script_path = positionals[0];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "        let script_src = match host_text::read_host_text(Path::new(script_path)) {"
          },
          {

            "text": "            Ok(s) =\u003e s,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "            Err(e) =\u003e {"
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "        };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "        let bin_path = Path::new(\".dev_shell.bin\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "        let vfs = match serialization::load_from_file(bin_path) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "            Ok(v) =\u003e v,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "            Err(e) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "                if e.kind() != io::ErrorKind::NotFound {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "                    let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"Failed to load {}: {}\", bin_path.display(), e);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "                }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "                Vfs::new()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "        };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "        let vfs = Rc::new(RefCell::new(vfs));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        let Ok(vm_session) = vm::try_session_rc(stderr) else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "            return Err(Box::new(std::io::Error::other(\"vm session\")));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "        };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "        if vm_session.borrow().is_guest_primary() {"

          {

            "text": "            if let Err(e) ="
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,

          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "            \u0026vfs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,

          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;RunWithError::Usage&#96; on invalid args; &#96;RunWithError::ReplFailed&#96; if the REPL exits with error."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "pub fn run_with\u003cR, W1, W2\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "    args: \u0026[String],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "    stdin: \u0026mut R,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "    stdout: \u0026mut W1,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "    stderr: \u0026mut W2,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": ") -\u003e Result\u003c(), RunWithError\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "where"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "    R: std::io::BufRead + std::io::Read,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "    W1: std::io::Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "    W2: std::io::Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "    let path = match args {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "        [] | [_] =\u003e Path::new(\".dev_shell.bin\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "        [_, path] =\u003e Path::new(path),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "        _ =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "            let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"usage: dev_shell [path]\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "            return Err(RunWithError::Usage);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "    };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "    let vfs = serialization::load_from_file(path).unwrap_or_default();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "    let vfs = Rc::new(RefCell::new(vfs));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "    let vm_session = vm::try_session_rc(stderr).map_err(|()| RunWithError::ReplFailed)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "    if vm_session.borrow().is_guest_primary() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "        if let Err(e) = session_store::apply_guest_primary_startup(\u0026mut vfs.borrow_mut(), path) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "            let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"dev_shell: guest-primary session: {e}\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "    repl::run(\u0026vfs, \u0026vm_session, false, path, stdin, stdout, stderr)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "        .map_err(|()| RunWithError::ReplFailed)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "#[cfg(test)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "mod tests;"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 10,

            "line": 166

      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "/// Save guest-primary session metadata (JSON). Does **not** write &#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "/// I/O errors from writing the JSON file."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "pub fn save_guest_primary(bin_path: \u0026Path, logical_cwd: \u0026str) -\u003e io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    let meta = GuestPrimarySessionV1::new(logical_cwd.to_string());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "    let text = serde_json::to_string_pretty(\u0026meta).map_err(|e| io::Error::other(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "    std::fs::write(session_path_for_bin(bin_path), text)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "/// Load guest-primary metadata if the companion file exists and is valid v1."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;Ok(None)&#96; if the file is missing or not a recognized format."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "/// I/O errors from reading the file, or invalid JSON (wrapped as &#96;InvalidData&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "pub fn load_guest_primary(bin_path: \u0026Path) -\u003e io::Result\u003cOption\u003cGuestPrimarySessionV1\u003e\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "    let p = session_path_for_bin(bin_path);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "    if !p.is_file() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "        return Ok(None);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "    let text = std::fs::read_to_string(\u0026p)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "    let v: GuestPrimarySessionV1 = serde_json::from_str(\u0026text).map_err(|e| {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "        io::Error::new("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "            io::ErrorKind::InvalidData,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "            format!(\"invalid guest-primary session JSON {}: {e}\", p.display()),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "    })?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "    if v.format != FORMAT_DEVSHELL_SESSION_V1 {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "        return Ok(None);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "    Ok(Some(v))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "/// On guest-primary startup: if a v1 session file exists, reset VFS to empty and restore &#96;logical_cwd&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "/// (creating directories as needed so [&#96;crate::devshell::vfs::Vfs::set_cwd&#96;] succeeds)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "/// If no session file exists, leaves &#96;vfs&#96; unchanged (e.g. legacy &#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96; load for cwd only)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "/// I/O from [&#96;load_guest_primary&#96;], or &#96;InvalidData&#96; if &#96;logical_cwd&#96; cannot be created or set."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "pub fn apply_guest_primary_startup("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "    vfs: \u0026mut crate::devshell::vfs::Vfs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "    bin_path: \u0026Path,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": ") -\u003e io::Result\u003c()\u003e"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 19,
            "line": 93
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 47
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/session_store.rs",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.510923445224762
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Session persistence for devshell (design §10)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "//! - **Mode S:** legacy [&#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96;] snapshot via [&#96;crate::devshell::serialization&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "//! - **Mode P (guest-primary):** the guest workspace is authoritative; we **do not** write the legacy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "//!   bin format on exit. Instead we persist **metadata only** (JSON) beside the bin path:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "//!   **&#96;{stem}.session.json&#96;** when the bin path ends in &#96;.bin&#96; (e.g. &#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96; →"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "//!   &#96;.dev_shell.session.json&#96;). The file records &#96;logical_cwd&#96; for the next REPL start; the in-memory"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "//!   VFS tree is not a second source of truth for the project tree in Mode P."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "use std::io;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "/// JSON &#96;format&#96; field for [&#96;GuestPrimarySessionV1&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "pub const FORMAT_DEVSHELL_SESSION_V1: \u0026str = \"devshell_session_v1\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "/// Metadata persisted for guest-primary sessions (no legacy VFS snapshot)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "pub struct GuestPrimarySessionV1 {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    pub format: String,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    /// Logical cwd (absolute Unix-style path) restored on startup."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    pub logical_cwd: String,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    /// Wall time when saved (milliseconds since UNIX epoch)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    pub saved_at_unix_ms: u64,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "impl GuestPrimarySessionV1 {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "    fn new(logical_cwd: String) -\u003e Self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "        let saved_at_unix_ms = SystemTime::now()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "            .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "            .map_or(0, |d| u64::try_from(d.as_millis()).unwrap_or(0));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        Self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "            format: FORMAT_DEVSHELL_SESSION_V1.to_string(),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "            logical_cwd,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "            saved_at_unix_ms,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "/// Companion path for guest-primary metadata next to a legacy &#96;.bin&#96; snapshot path."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "/// Example: &#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96; → &#96;.dev_shell.session.json&#96; (replaces the last extension)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "#[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "pub fn session_path_for_bin(bin_path: \u0026Path) -\u003e PathBuf {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "    bin_path.with_extension(\"session.json\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "/// Save guest-primary session metadata (JSON). Does **not** write &#96;.dev_shell.bin&#96;."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 50
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/session_store.rs",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.5040702223777771
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "/// On exit, the VFS is automatically saved to &#96;bin_path&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "pub fn run\u003cR, W1, W2\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "    vfs: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cVfs\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "    vm_session: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "    is_tty: bool,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "    bin_path: \u0026Path,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "    stdin: \u0026mut R,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "    stdout: \u0026mut W1,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "    stderr: \u0026mut W2,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": ") -\u003e Result\u003c(), ()\u003e"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "where"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "    R: BufRead + Read,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "    W1: Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    W2: Write,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "    if is_tty {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "        run_tty(vfs, vm_session, bin_path, stdin, stdout, stderr)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "    } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "        run_readline(vfs, vm_session, bin_path, stdin, stdout, stderr)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "fn save_on_exit\u003cW2: Write\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "    vfs: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cVfs\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "    vm_session: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "    bin_path: \u0026Path,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "    stderr: \u0026mut W2,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": ") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "    let cwd = vfs.borrow().cwd().to_string();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "        let mut vfs_mut = vfs.borrow_mut();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "    if vm_session.borrow().is_guest_primary() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "        let _ = writeln!("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,

          },

            "text": "        );"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "/// TTY branch: rustyline Editor with &#96;DevShellHelper&#96; (path completion via vfs)."

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "    let mut r = Cursor::new(bytes);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "    let mut magic = [0u8; 4];"

            "lineNumber": 169,

          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "    let mut ver = [0u8; 1];"

            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut ver).map_err(|_| Error::Truncated)?;"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "    let cwd_len = read_u32_le(\u0026mut r)?;"

          {
            "lineNumber": 179,

          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "    let mut cwd_buf = vec![0u8; cwd_len_usize];"

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "/// Save VFS to a .bin file."
          },

            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },

            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "/// Returns I/O error on serialize or write failure."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "pub fn save_to_file(vfs: \u0026Vfs, path: \u0026std::path::Path) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {

            "text": "    let bytes = serialize(vfs).map_err(std::io::Error::other)?;"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "/// Load VFS from a .bin file."

          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "/// Returns I/O error on read failure or invalid/corrupt .bin data."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! .bin format: magic \"DEVS\" + version + cwd + root node tree."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "use std::io::{Cursor, Read, Write};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "use super::vfs::{Node, Vfs};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "const MAGIC: \u0026[u8; 4] = b\"DEVS\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "const VERSION: u8 = 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "const NODE_DIR: u8 = 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "const NODE_FILE: u8 = 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "pub enum Error {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    InvalidMagic,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    InvalidVersion,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    Truncated,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    InvalidUtf8(std::string::FromUtf8Error),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "    Io(std::io::Error),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "impl std::fmt::Display for Error {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "            Self::InvalidMagic =\u003e write!(f, \"invalid magic\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "            Self::InvalidVersion =\u003e write!(f, \"invalid version\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "            Self::Truncated =\u003e write!(f, \"truncated data\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "            Self::InvalidUtf8(e) =\u003e write!(f, \"invalid utf-8: {e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "            Self::Io(e) =\u003e write!(f, \"io error: {e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "impl std::error::Error for Error {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    fn source(\u0026self) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "            Self::InvalidUtf8(e) =\u003e Some(e),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "            Self::Io(e) =\u003e Some(e),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "            _ =\u003e None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "impl From\u003cstd::io::Error\u003e for Error {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    fn from(e: std::io::Error) -\u003e Self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "        Self::Io(e)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "fn write_u32_le(w: \u0026mut impl Write, n: u32) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    w.write_all(\u0026n.to_le_bytes())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "fn write_u16_le(w: \u0026mut impl Write, n: u16) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    w.write_all(\u0026n.to_le_bytes())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "fn write_u64_le(w: \u0026mut impl Write, n: u64) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "    w.write_all(\u0026n.to_le_bytes())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "fn read_u32_le(r: \u0026mut impl Read) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003cu32\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "    let mut buf = [0u8; 4];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut buf)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "    Ok(u32::from_le_bytes(buf))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "fn read_u16_le(r: \u0026mut impl Read) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003cu16\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "    let mut buf = [0u8; 2];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70,
            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut buf)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "    Ok(u16::from_le_bytes(buf))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "fn read_u64_le(r: \u0026mut impl Read) -\u003e std::io::Result\u003cu64\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "    let mut buf = [0u8; 8];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut buf)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "    Ok(u64::from_le_bytes(buf))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "fn serialize_node"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 17,
            "line": 79
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/serialization.rs",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "    Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "fn deserialize_node(r: \u0026mut impl Read) -\u003e Result\u003cNode, Error\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "    let mut tag = [0u8; 1];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut tag).map_err(|e| {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "        if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "            Error::Truncated"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "        } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "            Error::Io(e)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "    })?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "    let name_len = read_u16_le(r)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "    let mut name_buf = vec![0u8; name_len as usize];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "    r.read_exact(\u0026mut name_buf)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "    let name = String::from_utf8(name_buf).map_err(Error::InvalidUtf8)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    match tag[0] {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "        NODE_DIR =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126,
            "text": "            let child_count = read_u32_le(r)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "            let n = usize::try_from(child_count).map_err(|_| Error::Truncated)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "            let mut children = Vec::with_capacity(n);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "            for _ in 0..child_count {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "                children.push(deserialize_node(r)?);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "            Ok(Node::Dir { name, children })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "        NODE_FILE =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "            let content_len = read_u64_le(r)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "            let n = usize::try_from(content_len).map_err(|_| Error::Truncated)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "            let mut content = vec![0u8; n];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "            r.read_exact(\u0026mut content)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "            Ok(Node::File { name, content })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "        _ =\u003e Err(Error::Truncated),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "/// Serialize VFS to .bin format: DEVS magic + version 1 + cwd + root node tree."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;Error::Io&#96; on write failure or if cwd/name/children length overflows the wire format."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "pub fn serialize(vfs: \u0026Vfs) -\u003e Result\u003cVec\u003cu8\u003e, Error\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "    let mut out = Vec::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "    out.write_all(MAGIC)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "    out.write_all(\u0026[VERSION])?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "    let cwd = vfs.cwd().as_bytes();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "    let cwd_len = u32::try_from(cwd.len())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "        .map_err(|_| Error::Io(std::io::Error::other(\"cwd len overflow\")))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "    write_u32_le(\u0026mut out, cwd_len)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "    out.write_all(cwd)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "    serialize_node(\u0026mut out, vfs.root())?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "    Ok(out)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "/// Deserialize VFS from .bin format."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "/// Returns &#96;Error&#96; on invalid magic/version, truncated data, or invalid UTF-8."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "pub fn deserialize(bytes: \u0026[u8]) -\u003e Result\u003cVfs, Error\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "    let mut r = Cursor::new(bytes);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "    let mut magic = [0u8; 4];"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": ")?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "#[cfg(test)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "mod tests {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "    use super::*;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "    use std::fs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "    fn tmp_dir(name: \u0026str) -\u003e PathBuf {"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "    #[test]"
          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "    #[test]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142,
            "text": "    fn roundtrip_guest_primary_session() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "        let dir = tmp_dir(\"roundtrip\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144,
            "text": "        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(\u0026dir);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "        fs::create_dir_all(\u0026dir).unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "        let bin_path = dir.join(\"state.bin\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,

            "lineNumber": 148,

          {

            "lineNumber": 151,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "    #[test]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "    fn apply_startup_sets_cwd() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "        let dir = tmp_dir(\"apply\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(\u0026dir);"

          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "        fs::create_dir_all(\u0026dir).unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160,
            "text": "        let session_path = session_path_for_bin(\u0026bin_path);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "        fs::write("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162,
            "text": "            \u0026session_path,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "            r#\"{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "            \"format\": \"devshell_session_v1\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "            \"logical_cwd\": \"/a/b\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "            \"saved_at_unix_ms\": 0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167,
            "text": "        }\"#,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169,
            "text": "        .unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "        let mut vfs = crate::devshell::vfs::Vfs::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171,
            "text": "        vfs.mkdir(\"/a/b\").unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "        vfs.write_file(\"/a/b/f\", b\"x\").unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173,
            "text": "        apply_guest_primary_startup(\u0026mut vfs, \u0026bin_path).unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "        assert_eq!(vfs.cwd(), \"/a/b\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175,
            "text": "        assert!(vfs.read_file(\"/a/b/f\").is_err());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "        let _ = fs::remove_dir_all(\u0026dir);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Export base directory resolution and &#96;PATH&#96; lookup."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "use std::path::PathBuf;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "/// Override parent directory for sandbox exports (&#96;devshell_*&#96; folders). Trims whitespace; empty ignores."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "pub const ENV_EXPORT_BASE: \u0026str = \"DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "/// Parent directory for per-run &#96;devshell_*&#96; export folders."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "/// Many Linux systems mount [&#96;std::env::temp_dir&#96;] (often &#96;/tmp&#96;) with **&#96;noexec&#96;**, so &#96;cargo run&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "/// can build but fails to **execute** &#96;target/debug/...&#96; with **Permission denied (EACCES)**. This"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "/// defaults to a user cache path that is normally executable."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "/// Resolution order:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "/// 1. **&#96;DEVSHELL_EXPORT_BASE&#96;** if set and non-empty."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "/// 2. Unix: **&#96;XDG_CACHE_HOME&#96;/cargo-devshell-exports**, else **&#96;HOME&#96;/.cache/cargo-devshell-exports**."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "/// 3. Windows: **&#96;LOCALAPPDATA&#96;/cargo-devshell-exports**."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "/// 4. Fall back to [&#96;std::env::temp_dir&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "#[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "pub fn devshell_export_parent_dir() -\u003e PathBuf {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    if let Ok(p) = std::env::var(ENV_EXPORT_BASE) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "        let p = p.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "        if !p.is_empty() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "            return PathBuf::from(p);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    #[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "        if let Ok(xdg) = std::env::var(\"XDG_CACHE_HOME\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "            let xdg = xdg.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "            if !xdg.is_empty() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "                return PathBuf::from(xdg).join(\"cargo-devshell-exports\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "        if let Ok(home) = std::env::var(\"HOME\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "            let home = home.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "            if !home.is_empty() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "                return PathBuf::from(home)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "                    .join(\".cache\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "                    .join(\"cargo-devshell-exports\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    #[cfg(windows)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "        if let Ok(local) = std::env::var(\"LOCALAPPDATA\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "            let local = local.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "            if !local.is_empty() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "                return PathBuf::from(local).join(\"cargo-devshell-exports\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "    std::env::temp_dir()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "/// Search for &#96;program&#96; in PATH. Returns the first absolute path where the executable exists."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 56
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/paths.rs",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.38892096281051636
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 449,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 450,
            "text": "        \"echo\" =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 451,
            "text": "            let line = argv[1..].join(\" \");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 452,
            "text": "            writeln!(stdout, \"{line}\").map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 453,
            "text": "            Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 454,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 455,
            "text": "        \"export-readonly\" | \"export_readonly\" =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 456,
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).map_or(\".\", String::as_str);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 457,
            "text": "            run_builtin_export_readonly(vfs, vm_session, stdout, path)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 458,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 459,
            "text": "        \"save\" =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 460,
            "text": "            let path = argv.get(1).map_or(\".dev_shell.bin\", String::as_str);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 461,
            "text": "            serialization::save_to_file(vfs, Path::new(path))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 462,
            "text": "                .map_err(|_| BuiltinError::SaveFailed)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 463,
            "text": "            Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 464,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 465,
            "text": "        \"todo\" =\u003e run_todo_cmd(stdout, stderr, argv),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 466,
            "text": "        \"rustup\" =\u003e run_rust_tool_builtin(vfs, vm_session, stderr, \"rustup\", argv),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 467,
            "text": "        \"cargo\" =\u003e run_rust_tool_builtin(vfs, vm_session, stderr, \"cargo\", argv),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 468,
            "text": "        \"help\" =\u003e run_builtin_help(stdout),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 469,
            "text": "        _ =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 470,
            "text": "            writeln!(stderr, \"unknown command: {name}\").map_err(|_| BuiltinError::RedirectWrite)?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 471,
            "text": "            Err(BuiltinError::UnknownCommand(name.to_string()))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 472,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 473,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 474,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 475
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 476,
            "text": "#[cfg(test)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 477,
            "text": "mod pipeline_limit_tests {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 478,
            "text": "    use super::*;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 479
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480,
            "text": "    #[test]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481,
            "text": "    fn pipeline_inter_stage_limit_boundary() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "        assert!(check_pipeline_inter_stage_size(PIPELINE_INTER_STAGE_MAX_BYTES).is_ok());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 483,
            "text": "        let e = check_pipeline_inter_stage_size(PIPELINE_INTER_STAGE_MAX_BYTES + 1).unwrap_err();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 484,
            "text": "        match e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 485,
            "text": "            BuiltinError::PipelineInterStageBufferExceeded { limit, actual } =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 486,
            "text": "                assert_eq!(limit, PIPELINE_INTER_STAGE_MAX_BYTES);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 487,
            "text": "                assert_eq!(actual, PIPELINE_INTER_STAGE_MAX_BYTES + 1);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 488,
            "text": "            }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489,
            "text": "            _ =\u003e panic!(\"expected PipelineInterStageBufferExceeded\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 491,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
