    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Sandbox: export VFS to a temp dir for isolated execution (e.g. rustup/cargo), then sync back."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "//! ## Isolation (no Podman/Docker)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "//! Devshell **does not** invoke &#96;podman&#96;, &#96;docker&#96;, or any OCI runtime. Flow: export VFS subtree to a"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "//! unique host temp dir (&#96;0o700&#96; on Unix) → run &#96;cargo&#96; / &#96;rustup&#96; from the host &#96;PATH&#96; with &#96;cwd&#96; set"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "//! to the export root → sync back → remove the temp dir."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "//! **Linux optional mount namespace** — set **&#96;DEVSHELL_RUST_MOUNT_NAMESPACE=1&#96;** (or &#96;true&#96; / &#96;yes&#96;) so the"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "//! child process calls &#96;unshare(CLONE_NEWNS)&#96; and makes the mount tree private (&#96;MS_REC | MS_PRIVATE&#96;)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "//! before &#96;exec&#96;. That gives a **separate mount namespace** (kernel feature via libc), similar in spirit"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "//! to container mount isolation but **without** a container engine. It does **not** hide the host"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "//! filesystem from the child; a full root jail would need additional work (e.g. &#96;pivot_root&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "//! On non-Linux platforms the env var is ignored."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "//! ## Unix execute bit on &#96;target/&#96; binaries"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "//! VFS sync uses [&#96;std::fs::write&#96;], which creates files without the execute bit. After a round-trip,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "//! &#96;target/debug/foo&#96; is often **0644** while still a valid ELF. &#96;cargo run&#96; may skip rebuild and then"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "//! **execve** fails with **EACCES (Permission denied)**. Before running &#96;cargo&#96;/&#96;rustup&#96;, we walk"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "//! &#96;target/&#96; and set **0755** on files that look like **ELF** objects."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "mod elf;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "mod error;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "mod export;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "mod paths;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "mod run;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "mod sync;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "#[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "mod linux_mount;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "#[cfg(test)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "mod tests;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "pub use error::SandboxError;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "pub use export::export_vfs_to_temp_dir;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "pub use paths::{devshell_export_parent_dir, find_in_path, ENV_EXPORT_BASE};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "pub use run::{run_in_export_dir, run_rust_tool};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "pub use sync::sync_host_dir_to_vfs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "pub(crate) use elf::restore_execute_bits_for_build_artifacts;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "pub(crate) use sync::host_export_root;"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 38,
            "line": 43
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/sandbox/mod.rs",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3798048496246338
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! &#96;RunResult&#96;, &#96;ExecContext&#96;, and &#96;BuiltinError&#96; for command execution."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "use super::super::vfs::Vfs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "use super::super::vm::SessionHolder;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "/// Result of running a pipeline: continue the REPL loop or exit."

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "/// Execution context: VFS and standard streams for one command."

          {

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },

          },
          {

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    RustupNotFound,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "    /// cargo not found in PATH"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    CargoNotFound,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    /// Sandbox export (VFS to temp dir) failed"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    SandboxExportFailed,"
          },
          {

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "pub fn apply_guest_primary_startup("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "    vfs: \u0026mut crate::devshell::vfs::Vfs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "    bin_path: \u0026Path,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": ") -\u003e io::Result\u003c()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "    let Some(meta) = load_guest_primary(bin_path)? else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "        return Ok(());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "    *vfs = crate::devshell::vfs::Vfs::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "    let cwd = meta.logical_cwd.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "    if cwd.is_empty() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "        return Ok(());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "    // set_cwd requires the path to exist as a directory in VFS — mkdir the chain first."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "    if let Err(e) = vfs.mkdir(cwd) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "        return Err(io::Error::new("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "            io::ErrorKind::InvalidData,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "            format!(\"session logical_cwd mkdir {cwd}: {e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "        ));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "    vfs.set_cwd(cwd).map_err(|e| {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "        io::Error::new("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "            io::ErrorKind::InvalidData,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "            format!(\"session logical_cwd set_cwd {cwd}: {e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "        )"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    })?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "    Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "#[cfg(test)]"

            "lineNumber": 120,

            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Devshell REPL and VFS: same logic as the &#96;cargo-devshell&#96; binary, exposed so tests can cover it."

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "mod repl;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "use std::cell::RefCell;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "use std::io::{self, BufReader, IsTerminal};"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "use std::rc::Rc;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "use vfs::Vfs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "/// Error from &#96;run_with&#96; (usage or REPL failure)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "pub enum RunWithError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    Usage,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    ReplFailed,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "impl std::fmt::Display for RunWithError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "            Self::Usage =\u003e f.write_str(\"usage error\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "            Self::ReplFailed =\u003e f.write_str(\"repl failed\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "impl std::error::Error for RunWithError {}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "/// Run the devshell using process args and standard I/O (for the binary)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "/// Returns an error if usage is wrong or I/O fails critically."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "pub fn run_main() -\u003e Result\u003c(), Box\u003cdyn std::error::Error\u003e\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "    let args: Vec\u003cString\u003e = std::env::args().collect();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "    let is_tty = io::stdin().is_terminal();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    let mut stdin = BufReader::new(io::stdin());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    let mut stdout = io::stdout();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    let mut stderr = io::stderr();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "    run_main_from_args(\u0026args, is_tty, \u0026mut stdin, \u0026mut stdout, \u0026mut stderr)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "/// Same as &#96;run_main&#96; but takes args, &#96;is_tty&#96;, and streams (for tests and callers that supply I/O)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "/// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "/// Returns an error if usage is wrong or I/O fails critically."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "pub fn run_main_from_args\u003cR, W1, W2\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "    args: \u0026[String],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    is_tty: bool,"
          }
        ],

          "endPosition": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! REPL: read-eval-print loop with &#96;parse_line&#96; and &#96;execute_pipeline&#96;; handles exit/quit."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "//!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "//! TTY: rustyline Editor with path completion (&#96;CompletionType::List&#96;, bash-like);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "//! non-TTY: &#96;read_line&#96; loop."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "//! On exit (exit/quit or EOF), VFS is auto-saved to &#96;bin_path&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "//! Shared loop body is in &#96;process_line&#96; so it can be unit-tested."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "use std::cell::RefCell;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "use std::io::{BufRead, Read, Write};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "use std::path::Path;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "use std::rc::Rc;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "use rustyline::config::Configurer;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "use rustyline::{CompletionType, Editor};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "use super::command::{execute_pipeline, ExecContext, RunResult};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "use super::completion::DevShellHelper;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "use super::parser;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "use super::script;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "use super::serialization;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "use super::session_store;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "use super::vfs::Vfs;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "use super::vm::SessionHolder;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "/// Result of processing one REPL line: continue the loop or exit."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "pub enum StepResult {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    Continue,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    Exit,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "/// Process one input line: parse, optionally run pipeline, return whether to exit."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "/// Handles &#96;source path&#96; and &#96;. path&#96; by loading script text via [&#96;crate::devshell::script::read_script_source_text&#96;]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "/// (workspace guest / VFS, then host) and running it."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "/// Used by both TTY and non-TTY loops; exposed for tests."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "pub fn process_line\u003cR, W1, W2\u003e("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    vfs: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cVfs\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "    vm_session: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    line: \u0026str,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    stdin: \u0026mut R"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 17,
            "line": 39
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./crates/todo/src/devshell/repl.rs",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.36971598863601685
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Tests for &#96;run_main_from_args&#96;: usage error, file not found, failed to load, script mode."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "use std::io::Cursor;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "use super::super::run_main_from_args;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "#[test]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "fn run_main_from_args_usage_error() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "    let args = vec![\"a\".to_string(), \"b\".to_string(), \"c\".to_string()];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    let mut stdin = Cursor::new(\"\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    let mut stdout = Vec::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    let mut stderr = Vec::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    let r = run_main_from_args(\u0026args, false, \u0026mut stdin, \u0026mut stdout, \u0026mut stderr);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "    assert!(r.is_err());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    let err = String::from_utf8(stderr).unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    assert!(err.contains(\"usage\"));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "#[test]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "fn run_main_from_args_file_not_found() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    let path = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!(\"devshell_nonexist_{}\", std::process::id()));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "    let args = vec![\"dev_shell\".to_string(), path.display().to_string()];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    let mut stdin = Cursor::new(\"exit\\n\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    let mut stdout = Vec::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    let mut stderr = Vec::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    let r = run_main_from_args(\u0026args, false, \u0026mut stdin, \u0026mut stdout, \u0026mut stderr);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    r.unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    let err = String::from_utf8(stderr).unwrap();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    assert!(err.contains(\"File not found\") || err.is_empty());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31

下面分三块说明：**为什么会报错**、**和「统一成一份」的设计差在哪里**、**可以怎么实现**。

---

## 1. 报错原因（不是「少删了一个文件」这么简单）

- **`cargo new --bin hello` 是在 Lima 虚拟机里执行的**，工作目录在 guest 上一般是 **`/workspace`**（或子目录），对应设计里的 **guest 真源 / Mode P**。
- 这块空间在物理上来自 **宿主上的 `workspace_parent`**（例如 `session_gamma::workspace_parent_for_instance` → `…/devshell-export/vm-workspace/<实例名>/`），通过 Lima **挂载**到 guest 的 `/workspace`。
- 你删的是仓库里的 **`.dev_shell.bin`**（以及可能还有 **`.dev_shell.session.json`**）。这只影响：
  - 是否从旧格式加载 **内存 VFS 快照**；
  - guest-primary 时是否从 **session JSON** 里恢复 `logical_cwd`（见 `session_store::apply_guest_primary_startup`）。
- **它不会删除** `workspace_parent` 里已经生成过的 **`hello/` 目录**。所以 guest 里 **`/workspace/hello` 仍然存在**，`cargo new` 就会报 `destination ... already exists`。

也就是说：**工程树的真源在 Mode P 下已经是 guest（挂载目录）**；删 `.dev_shell.bin` **不等于**清空那块磁盘。

---

## 2. 和「两者统一成一份」的设计差在哪里

`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-20-devshell-guest-primary-design.md` **§10** 已经说得很清楚：

- **Mode P 不再用 legacy `.dev_shell.bin` 表示工程树**；
- 会话持久化应是 **与 guest 虚拟工作区一致** 的载体（目前实现里是 **`.dev_shell.session.json` 只存元数据，例如 `logical_cwd`**）。

当前缺口是：

- **元数据（session）** 和 **挂载目录里的真实文件** 没有 **「同生共死」的契约**：
  - 删了宿主侧 session/bin，**没有**定义「要对 `workspace_parent` 或 guest `/workspace` 做什么」；
  - 所以会出现：**会话像「新开」，磁盘仍是「旧项目」**。

这就是你看到的 **不一致**：不是 VM「没同步删」，而是 **设计上还没规定「宿主删会话 = 清空工作区」**。

---

## 3. 功能上可以怎么实现（推荐从易到难）

### A. 产品行为（推荐先做）

1. **明确文档**：说明 **`.dev_shell.bin` / `.dev_shell.session.json` 不负责删除 VM 工作区**；清工程树要清 **`DEVSHELL_VM_WORKSPACE_PARENT` 指向的目录**（或文档里写默认路径规则）。
2. **启动时检测「孤儿工作区」**（可选）：  
   - 若 **guest-primary** 且 **没有**有效 session（或显式「新会话」），而 `workspace_parent` 非空，则 **stderr 提示**：「检测到已有工作区文件；若需全新环境请使用 `--reset-workspace` 或手动删除某路径」—— **不自动删**，避免误删。

### B. 显式「重置工作区」（推荐实现）

- 增加 **`cargo-devshell` / `dev_shell` 参数** 或环境变量，例如：  
  `--reset-workspace` 或 `DEVSHELL_VM_RESET_WORKSPACE=1`  
- 实现上 **只允许** 清理 **`workspace_parent_for_instance(instance)`** 下的内容（即当前 Lima 实例绑定的那棵目录），执行前可要求 **`--yes`** 或交互确认。
- 实现方式二选一或组合：  
  - 宿主：`std::fs::remove_dir_all` / 清空子项；  
  - 或 `limactl shell <instance> -- sh -lc 'rm -rf /workspace/*'`（需确认与挂载一致）。

这样 **「删 .dev_shell.bin」和「清 VM 工作区」** 在用户心智上可以 **通过一条命令对齐**，而不是偷偷自动删。

### C. 会话与磁盘「绑定代数」（进阶，真正「统一」）

- 在 **`.dev_shell.session.json`**（或单独文件）里增加 **`workspace_epoch` / `session_id`（UUID）**。
- 在 **guest `/workspace`（或宿主 `workspace_parent`）根** 写一个 **` .devshell_epoch`**，内容相同。
- **启动时**：  
  - 若宿主 **无 session** 但 guest **有 epoch 文件** → 判定为 **孤儿** → 走 **B** 的提示或仅允许 `cargo new` 前提示；  
  - 若宿主 **新 session 新 epoch** → 可选：**仅在用户确认后** 清空旧 `workspace_parent` 再写入新 epoch。

这样 **「一份真源」** 在工程上变成：**session 元数据 + 工作区目录由同一 `epoch` 关联**，删 session 时可定义策略（仅提示 / 可选自动清目录）。

### D. 长期（结构性）

- **dispatch 全面走 `WorkspaceBackend`**（设计 §4.2），所有 `ls`/`cd`/`cargo` 都只看到 **同一后端**，宿主不再维护一份「可对不上的」内存树 —— 这是大改，但能从根本上减少双份状态。

---

## 小结

| 现象 | 原因 |
|------|------|
| 删了 `.dev_shell.bin` 仍报 `hello` 已存在 | 工程树在 **Lima 挂载目录**里，**未被**删 bin 删除 |
| 与设计「统一一份」的差距 | **会话文件**与 **workspace 磁盘**缺少 **epoch/重置策略** |

**最小可行**：**B（显式 reset）+ A（文档/告警）**；**完整统一**：再加 **C（epoch 绑定）**。

如果你希望我在仓库里 **落地一版**（例如 `--reset-workspace` + 启动时孤儿检测 + 文档），可以说一下倾向：**仅清 `workspace_parent`、是否默认要确认、是否写 epoch 文件**。

---

_**User**_

增加这么多说明和命令参数，太乱了。还是需要简单一些，请将两者统一成一个。例如：运行 cargo run --bin cargo-devshell 时，在启动的时候，先启动虚拟机，虚拟机成功启动后。便可以获得其所用的虚拟文件系统，记录下其全局路径。所有内部命令，在虚拟机启动时，是都不需要运行的。直接使用 ssh shell 即可。只有，虚拟机不运行的情况下，才需要使用内部命令，这些内部命令都应修改为直接操作这个虚拟机使用的虚拟文件系统。

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Syncing incremental changes**

I'm investigating how `push_incremental` from host VFS might break due to resolve_absolute calls. 

**Copying tree to host**

I'm copying tree structures from VFS to host directories using `copy_tree_to_host`. I'm fixing `logical_to_host_path` to handle errors gracefully instead of panicking. I'm handling edge cases in `logical_to_host_path` for empty paths and root directory serialization. 

**Updating path conversion**

I'm removing the `expect` from `logical_to_host_path` and making it private. </details></think>

已按计划把「虚拟机交互」和「宿主离线」两条路径收束，并完成编译与测试。摘要如下：

## 行为统一

1. **`devshell/mod.rs`**  
   - Unix 启动时调用 **`export_devshell_workspace_root_env()`**，把 Lima 工作区父目录的规范路径写入 **`DEVSHELL_WORKSPACE_ROOT`**。  
   - **`try_session_rc_or_host`**：γ 初始化失败时退回 **Host**，不再直接报错退出。  
   - **交互 + TTY + 非 `-f`**：在 **`#[cfg(all(unix, not(test)))]`** 下若 **`should_delegate_lima_shell`** 为真，则 `ensure_ready` 后 **`exec_lima_interactive_shell`**（不再用内置 REPL）。  
   - **否则**：若 **`is_host_only()`** 且非测试，用 **`Vfs::new_host_root(vm_workspace_host_root())`**；否则仍从 **`.dev_shell.bin`** 加载。  
   - **`-f` 脚本** 与 **`run_with`** 同样按上述规则选 VFS。

2. **`repl.rs`**  
   - **`vfs.is_host_backed()`** 时退出不再写 **`.dev_shell.bin`**，并打印说明（依赖 **`DEVSHELL_WORKSPACE_ROOT`**）。

3. **`vm/mod.rs`**  
   - 按 clippy 要求把 **`Error::new(Other, …)`** 改为 **`Error::other(…)`**。

4. **`docs/devshell-vm-gamma.md`**  
   - 增加 **「简单心智模型」** 与 **`DEVSHELL_WORKSPACE_ROOT` / `DEVSHELL_VM_INTERNAL_REPL`** 表格说明。

## 验证

- `cargo clippy -p xtask-todo-lib --all-targets -- -D warnings`  
- `cargo test -p xtask-todo-lib`（248 个测试通过，1 个 ignored）

**说明**：单测里 **`cfg(test)`** 仍用内存 VFS；**`not(test)`** 保证交互式委托不会在 CI 里 `exec` 掉测试进程。

_**User**_

@\home\ars\.cursor\projects\home-ars-richard-2026-pvm-2-xtask-todo\terminals\12.txt:1053-1060 报错了。请分析。

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


## 原因说明

报错 **`linker 'cc' not found`** 表示：在 **Lima 虚拟机里的 Linux 环境**中，Rust 在链接阶段需要调用系统的 **C 链接器**（通常通过 **`cc`** 这个命令，一般由 **`gcc`** 或 **`clang`** 提供），但当前 **guest 里没有安装** 这类工具链，所以 `cargo` 找不到可执行的 `cc`。

这和「Rust 是否用 rustup 装好」是两件事：**`rustc`/`cargo` 有了，若没有 C 编译器/链接器，很多原生 crate 或最终可执行文件的链接仍会失败**。

## 处理办法（在 guest 里执行）

在 **Lima 的 shell**（你现在的 `lima-devshell-rust` 会话）里安装构建工具，例如 **Debian/Ubuntu 系**：

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y build-essential
```

装好后在同一目录再执行：

```bash
cargo run
```

若是 **Fedora/RHEL** 系，可用：

```bash
sudo dnf install gcc
```

（或 `gcc-c++` / `clang` 等，视发行版而定。）

## 小结

| 现象 | 含义 |
|------|------|
| `linker 'cc' not found` | guest 缺少用于链接的 C 工具链（`cc`/`gcc`） |
| 解决 | 在 VM 内安装 `build-essential`（或等价包） |

这与本仓库 `cargo-devshell` 的挂载或工作区路径无关，是 **guest 系统软件包不完整** 导致的。

---

_**User**_

不能使用系统的么？之前 rust 工具链都是使用系统的 rust 工具链的。

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


可以，而且**本来就应该用「系统里装的」C 工具链**，这和「Rust 用系统包还是用 rustup」是两件事。

## 为什么还会缺 `cc`？

- **Rust（`rustc` / `cargo`）**：可以来自 **系统包**（如 `apt install rustc cargo`）或 **rustup**。
- **链接器 `cc`**：一般来自 **系统里的 `gcc` / `clang`**（Debian/Ubuntu 里常打包成 **`build-essential`**），**Rust 默认不会自带一整套 C 编译器**，所以即使用「系统 Rust」，只要走常规 GNU 链接，**仍然要在系统里装 `gcc`（或等价物）**。

所以你看到的报错，不是「没用系统 Rust」，而是：**当前这个 VM 里只装了/挂好了 Rust，没有装用于链接的 C 工具链**。

## 「用系统的」具体怎么做？

在 **guest 里**用系统包管理器装上即可，这就是标准做法：

```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y build-essential
```

（Fedora 等用 `dnf install gcc` 等。）

装好后，`cargo` 会调用系统里的 `cc`/`gcc`，**不需要**再单独「换一套 Rust」。

## 若你之前是「宿主挂载的 Rust」

文档里那种 **只读挂载宿主 `~/.rustup`、`~/.cargo`** 的方式，只是把 **Rust 可执行文件和工具链** 放进 guest；**链接步骤仍在 guest 里执行**，所以 **guest 里照样要有 `gcc`/`cc`**（或通过环境变量显式指定别的链接器）。  
想尽量少装包，只能走 **musl / 自带 lld** 等特殊目标，一般小项目直接装 **`build-essential` 最省事**。

**结论**：可以继续用你现在的「系统 Rust」或挂载的 Rust；**再在同一套系统里装上 `build-essential`（或 `gcc`）**，就是「使用系统的」链接器，和之前并不矛盾。

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Mounting toolchain**

I'm implementing a partial /usr/bin mount for VM toolchain sharing, avoiding direct file mounts due to Lima limitations. I'll mount /usr/bin subset to /host-gcc-bin read-only and update PATH accordingly. 

**Analyzing library loading**

The guest binary loads libc from guest environment, not host, due to RPATH and dynamic linking. 

**Mounting host usr**

I'm mounting host /usr as read-only to /host-usr to avoid invasive GCC prefix changes. I need to mount the entire /usr directory from host to /host-usr to properly handle gcc's hardcoded /usr prefix. I need to determine if gcc searches relative paths from its own location or uses hardcoded prefixes. 

**Analyzing gcc search paths**

I found that gcc uses install prefix paths and `gcc -print-search-dirs` shows paths relative to `/usr` for Ubuntu packages. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


说明：`cc`/`gcc` 不是单一可执行文件，会调用 `cc1`、`ld`、`as` 等，且发行版自带的 gcc 常写死 `/usr/lib/gcc/...` 路径。正在添加实验性文档与可复制的 Lima 片段（推荐自包含前缀如 `/opt/...`，而非只挂单个 `cc`）。

## 结论：不能只读挂载「一个 `cc`」

在仓库里加了一份**说明 + 示例片段**，说明原因和可行做法：

1. **新建** [`docs/snippets/lima-devshell-host-cc-mount.yaml`](docs/snippets/lima-devshell-host-cc-mount.yaml)  
   - 说明为什么**只挂** `/usr/bin/cc` 或单个 `gcc` **通常不够**（还要 `cc1`、`ld`、`as` 和 `libgcc` 等，且常见发行版 **gcc 写死 `/usr/lib/gcc/...`**）。  
   - **相对可行**的做法：在**宿主机**先装**一整棵、单前缀**工具链（例如 **`/opt/my-cc`**），再**整目录只读**挂到 guest（示例里用 `mountPoint: /host-toolchain`），并把 **`PATH`** 加上 **`/host-toolchain/bin`**（若同时挂 Rust，与 `lima-devshell-rust-toolchain-mount.yaml` 里的 **`PATH`** 合并，别把 `host-cargo` 顶掉）。

2. **更新** [`docs/devshell-vm-gamma.md`](docs/devshell-vm-gamma.md)  
   - 增加 **「做法三（实验性）：只读挂载宿主 C 编译器」**  
   - 在 **「限制与排错」** 里加了 **`linker 'cc' not found`** 的条目。

**说明**：我没有在仓库里写「自动安装宿主 `/opt/my-cc`」的脚本——那取决于你想用 **gcc 套件、LLVM、还是 musl/cross**，需要你在宿主机自己装好后，再把**那一棵目录**挂进 VM。

**若不想折腾前缀**：在 guest 里执行 **`sudo apt install -y build-essential`** 仍然是最省事、最稳的「用系统 C 工具链」方式。

---

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


已按「在 guest 里用 apt 装 C 工具链」为主改完文档，变更如下。

## 1. `docs/devshell-vm-gamma.md`

- **「做法三」** 改为：**在 guest 内安装 C 链接器（推荐）**，给出：
  - `sudo apt update`
  - `sudo apt install -y build-essential`
- 说明：`cargo build` 需要系统里的 `cc`/`gcc`，与 Rust 是否来自 rustup/挂载无关。
- 补充验证与非 Debian（如 Fedora）的示例命令。
- **「限制与排错」** 里 **`linker 'cc' not found`** 只指向上述 **`apt install build-essential`** 和「做法三」，不再提从宿主挂载。

## 2. `docs/snippets/lima-devshell-host-cc-mount.yaml`

- 收束为几条注释：**推荐用 guest 里的 `apt install build-essential`**；并注明不要只挂宿主单个 `cc`；高级 `/opt` 整目录挂载仅作一句说明。

之后在 VM 里遇到缺 `cc`，按 **做法三** 执行即可。

分析代码中 VM 就绪流程，确定在何处加入对 `gcc`/`build-essential` 的检测与（在可行时）自动安装。

            "text": "    pub fn shutdown(\u0026mut self, vfs: \u0026mut Vfs, vfs_cwd: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "            Self::Host(s) =\u003e VmExecutionSession::shutdown(s, vfs, vfs_cwd),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "            #[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185,
            "text": "            Self::Gamma(s) =\u003e VmExecutionSession::shutdown(s, vfs, vfs_cwd),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "            #[cfg(all(unix, feature = \"beta-vm\"))]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187,
            "text": "            Self::Beta(s) =\u003e VmExecutionSession::shutdown(s, vfs, vfs_cwd),"
          },

            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191,
            "text": "    /// When γ is in guest-primary mode ([&#96;WorkspaceMode::Guest&#96;]), returns the session for direct guest FS ops."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "    ///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "    /// Returns &#96;None&#96; for host sandbox, β, or Mode S γ (push/pull sync)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "    #[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "    #[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "    pub fn guest_primary_gamma_mut(\u0026mut self) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026mut GammaSession\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "            Self::Gamma(g) if !g.syncs_vfs_with_host_workspace() =\u003e Some(g),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199,
            "text": "            _ =\u003e None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203,
            "text": "    /// γ **or** β guest-primary: [&#96;GuestFsOps&#96;] + guest mount for [&#96;logical_path_to_guest&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "    ///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205,
            "text": "    /// Returns &#96;None&#96; for host sandbox, Mode S sync, or non–guest-primary sessions."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "    /// Mount is owned so the returned trait object does not alias a borrow of the session."

            "text": "    }"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "    fn ensure_session_started(\u0026mut self) -\u003e Result\u003c(), VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "        if self.session_started {"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,

          },
          {

          },

            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "        })?;"
          },

            "lineNumber": 144,

            "lineNumber": 151,

            "lineNumber": 152,

            "lineNumber": 156,

            "lineNumber": 157,

          {

            "lineNumber": 160,

          },

            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "        self.session_started = true;"

            "lineNumber": 164
          },

      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "fn guest_dir_for_cwd_inner(guest_mount: \u0026str, vfs_cwd: \u0026str) -\u003e String {"

            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "impl GammaSession {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "    /// Build a γ session from VM config (does not start the VM yet)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "    ///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "    /// # Errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "    /// Returns [&#96;VmError::Lima&#96;] if &#96;limactl&#96; cannot be resolved."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "    pub fn new(config: \u0026VmConfig) -\u003e Result\u003cSelf, VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "        let limactl = resolve_limactl()?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "        let workspace_parent = workspace_parent_for_instance(\u0026config.lima_instance);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "        let guest_mount = std::env::var(ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_GUEST_WORKSPACE)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "            .ok()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "            .map(|s| s.trim().to_string())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122,
            "text": "            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"/workspace\".to_string());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "        let sync_vfs_with_workspace ="
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "            matches!(config.workspace_mode_effective(), WorkspaceMode::Sync);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "        Ok(Self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "            lima_instance: config.lima_instance.clone(),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129,
            "text": "            workspace_parent,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "            guest_mount,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "            limactl,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132,
            "text": "            vm_started: false,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "            lima_hints_checked: false,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "            sync_vfs_with_workspace,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "        })"

            "lineNumber": 136,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "impl std::fmt::Display for VmError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "    fn fmt(\u0026self, f: \u0026mut std::fmt::Formatter\u003c'_\u003e) -\u003e std::fmt::Result {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "            Self::Sandbox(e) =\u003e write!(f, \"{e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63,
            "text": "            Self::Sync(e) =\u003e write!(f, \"{e}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "            Self::BackendNotImplemented(s) =\u003e write!(f, \"vm backend not implemented: {s}\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65,
            "text": "            Self::Lima(s) =\u003e f.write_str(s),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "            Self::Ipc(s) =\u003e f.write_str(s),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 68,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 69,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71,
            "text": "impl std::error::Error for VmError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "    fn source(\u0026self) -\u003e Option\u003c\u0026(dyn std::error::Error + 'static)\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "            Self::Sandbox(e) =\u003e Some(e),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "            Self::Sync(e) =\u003e Some(e),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76,
            "text": "            Self::BackendNotImplemented(_) | Self::Lima(_) | Self::Ipc(_) =\u003e None,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "/// Abstraction for a devshell execution session (host temp dir, γ VM, or β sidecar)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "pub trait VmExecutionSession {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "    /// Prepare the session (e.g. start VM, initial push). No-op for host temp export."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "/// Active VM / sandbox backend for one REPL or script run."

            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "pub enum SessionHolder {"

          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "fn truthy_env(key: \u0026str) -\u003e bool {"

          },

            "lineNumber": 53
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "fn resolve_limactl() -\u003e Result\u003cPathBuf, VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {

            "text": "    if let Ok(p) = std::env::var(ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_LIMACTL) {"

          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "/// Host workspace root shared with β (&#96;session_start.staging_dir&#96;)."

            "text": "///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "#[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,

          },

          {

            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "/// Guest working directory for VFS cwd (mount + last path segment)."

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 264,
            "text": "    /// Replace this process with an interactive &#96;limactl shell&#96; (&#96;bash -l&#96;) under the guest workspace mount."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 265,
            "text": "    ///"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 266,
            "text": "    /// On success, does not return. On failure, returns the [&#96;std::io::Error&#96;] from [&#96;std::os::unix::process::CommandExt::exec&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267,
            "text": "    #[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 268,
            "text": "    pub fn exec_lima_interactive_shell(\u0026self) -\u003e std::io::Error {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 269,
            "text": "        use std::os::unix::process::CommandExt;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 270,
            "text": "        use std::process::Command;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 271,
            "text": "        match self {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 272,
            "text": "            Self::Gamma(g) =\u003e Command::new(g.limactl_path())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 273,
            "text": "                .arg(\"shell\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 274,
            "text": "                .arg(\"--workdir\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 275,
            "text": "                .arg(g.guest_mount())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 276,
            "text": "                .arg(g.lima_instance_name())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 277,
            "text": "                .arg(\"--\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 278,
            "text": "                .arg(\"bash\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 279,
            "text": "                .arg(\"-l\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 280,
            "text": "                .exec(),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 281,
            "text": "            _ =\u003e std::io::Error::other("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 282,
            "text": "                \"exec_lima_interactive_shell: not a Lima gamma session\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 283,
            "text": "            ),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 284,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 285,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 286,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 287
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 288,
            "text": "/// Build [&#96;SessionHolder&#96;] from the environment. On failure (e.g. default γ Lima but &#96;limactl&#96; missing), writes to &#96;stderr&#96; and returns &#96;Err(())&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 289,
            "text": "/// Use **&#96;DEVSHELL_VM=off&#96;** or **&#96;DEVSHELL_VM_BACKEND=host&#96;** to force the host temp sandbox."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 290,
            "text": "#[allow(clippy::result_unit_err)] // binary entry uses &#96;()&#96;; message already on stderr"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 291,
            "text": "pub fn try_session_rc(stderr: \u0026mut dyn Write) -\u003e Result\u003cRc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e, ()\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 292,
            "text": "    let config = VmConfig::from_env();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 293,
            "text": "    match SessionHolder::try_from_config(\u0026config) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 294,
            "text": "        Ok(s) =\u003e Ok(Rc::new(RefCell::new(s))),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 295,
            "text": "        Err(e) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 296,
            "text": "            let _ = writeln!(stderr, \"dev_shell: {e}\");"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 297,
            "text": "            Err(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 298,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 299,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 300,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 301
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 302,
            "text": "/// Like [&#96;try_session_rc&#96;], but on failure uses [&#96;SessionHolder::Host&#96;] so the REPL can run against"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 303,
            "text": "/// [&#96;session_gamma::workspace_parent_for_instance&#96;] (same tree as the Lima mount)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 304,
            "text": "pub fn try_session_rc_or_host(stderr: \u0026mut dyn Write) -\u003e Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "    match try_session_rc(stderr) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "        Ok(s) =\u003e s,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 307,
            "text": "        Err(()) =\u003e"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "lineNumber": 259,
            "text": "    pub fn guest_mount(\u0026self) -\u003e \u0026str {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 262
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 263,
            "text": "    fn limactl_stop(\u0026self) -\u003e Result\u003c(), VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 264,
            "text": "        let st = Command::new(\u0026self.limactl)"

          {
            "lineNumber": 265,
            "text": "            .args([\"stop\", \u0026self.lima_instance])"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 266,
            "text": "            .stdin(Stdio::null())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267,
            "text": "            .stdout(Stdio::inherit())"

            "text": "            .stderr(Stdio::inherit())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 269,
            "text": "            .status()"
          },

            "text": "            .map_err(|e| VmError::Lima(format!(\"limactl stop: {e}\")))?;"
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 272,
            "text": "            return Err(VmError::Lima(format!("

          },
          {

            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 280
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 281,

      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "//! Optional session-scoped VM execution (γ CLI / β sidecar): host [&#96;SessionHolder::Host&#96;], Unix γ [&#96;SessionHolder::Gamma&#96;]."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {

            "text": "#![allow(clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {

            "text": "use std::cell::RefCell;"

          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 8
          },

          },

            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },

          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "pub use config::{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "    workspace_mode_from_env, VmConfig, WorkspaceMode, ENV_DEVSHELL_VM, ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_BACKEND,"
          },
          {

          },

          },

          {

            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "pub use guest_fs_ops::LimaGuestFsOps;"
          },

            "text": "pub use guest_fs_ops::{"

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "    GuestFsOps, MockGuestFsOps,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "pub use lima_diagnostics::ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_LIMA_HINTS;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "pub use session_gamma::{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "    workspace_parent_for_instance, GammaSession, ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_GUEST_WORKSPACE,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_LIMACTL, ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_STOP_ON_EXIT, ENV_DEVSHELL_VM_WORKSPACE_PARENT,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "pub use session_host::HostSandboxSession;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "pub use sync::{pull_workspace_to_vfs, push_full, push_incremental, VmSyncError};"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "use std::process::ExitStatus;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "use super::sandbox;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "/// Errors from VM session operations."

          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "#[derive(Debug)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "pub enum VmError {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "    Sandbox(sandbox::SandboxError),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "    Sync(VmSyncError),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    /// Backend not implemented on this OS or not wired yet."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    BackendNotImplemented(\u0026'static str),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "    /// Lima / &#96;limactl&#96; or γ orchestration failure (message for stderr)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "    Lima(String),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    /// β IPC / &#96;devshell-vm&#96; protocol failure."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "    Ipc(String),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "impl std::fmt::Display for VmError {"
          }
        ],

      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 221,
            "text": "            ))),"

            "lineNumber": 222,
            "text": "        }"

          {
            "lineNumber": 223,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 225
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 226,
            "text": "impl VmExecutionSession for BetaSession {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 227,
            "text": "    fn ensure_ready(\u0026mut self, _vfs: \u0026Vfs, _vfs_cwd: \u0026str) -\u003e Result\u003c(), VmError\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 228,
            "text": "        if self.handshake_ok {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 229,
            "text": "            return Ok(());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 230,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 231,
            "text": "        let stream = self.conn()?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 232,
            "text": "        let req = serde_json::json!({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 233,
            "text": "            \"op\": \"handshake\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 234,
            "text": "            \"version\": 1u64,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 235,
            "text": "            \"client\": \"cargo-devshell\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 236,
            "text": "            \"client_version\": env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\"),"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 237,
            "text": "        });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 238,
            "text": "        let line = serde_json::to_string(\u0026req).map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 239,
            "text": "        writeln!(stream, \"{line}\").map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 240,
            "text": "        stream.flush().map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 241,
            "text": "        let mut reader = BufReader::new("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 242,
            "text": "            stream"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 243,
            "text": "                .try_clone()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 244,
            "text": "                .map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(format!(\"stream clone: {e}\")))?,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 245,
            "text": "        );"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 246,
            "text": "        let mut out = String::new();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 247,
            "text": "        reader"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 248,
            "text": "            .read_line(\u0026mut out)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 249,
            "text": "            .map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 250,
            "text": "        let v: serde_json::Value ="
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 251,
            "text": "            serde_json::from_str(out.trim()).map_err(|e| VmError::Ipc(e.to_string()))?;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 252,
            "text": "        if v.get(\"op\").and_then(|x| x.as_str()) != Some(\"handshake_ok\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 253,
            "text": "            return Err(VmError::Ipc(format!(\"handshake: unexpected {v}\")));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 254,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 255,
            "text": "        self.handshake_ok = true;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 256,
            "text": "        Ok(())"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 257,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 258
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 259,
            "text": "    fn run_rust_tool("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 260,
            "text": "        \u0026mut self,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 261,
            "text": "        vfs: \u0026mut Vfs,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 262,
            "text": "        vfs_cwd: \u0026str"
          }
        ],
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 304,
            "text": " Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "    match try_session_rc(stderr) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "        Ok(s) =\u003e s,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 307,
            "text": "        Err(()) =\u003e {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 308,
            "text": "            let _ = writeln!("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 309,
            "text": "                stderr,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 310,
            "text": "                \"dev_shell: VM unavailable — in-process REPL uses the same host directory as the Lima workspace (DEVSHELL_WORKSPACE_ROOT).\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 311,
            "text": "            );"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 312,
            "text": "            Rc::new(RefCell::new(SessionHolder::Host(HostSandboxSession::new())))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 313,
            "text": "        }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 314,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 315,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317,
            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "pub fn export_devshell_workspace_root_env() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319,
            "text": "    let c = config::VmConfig::from_env();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "    let p = session_gamma::workspace_parent_for_instance(\u0026c.lima_instance);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 321,
            "text": "    let _ = std::fs::create_dir_all(\u0026p);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 322,
            "text": "    if let Ok(can) = p.canonicalize() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 323,
            "text": "        std::env::set_var(\"DEVSHELL_WORKSPACE_ROOT\", can.as_os_str());"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 324,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 325,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 326
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 327,
            "text": "#[cfg(not(unix))]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 328,
            "text": "pub fn export_devshell_workspace_root_env() {}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 329
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 330,
            "text": "/// Host directory that Lima mounts at the guest workspace (e.g. &#96;/workspace&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 331,
            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 332,
            "text": "#[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 333,
            "text": "pub fn vm_workspace_host_root() -\u003e std::path::PathBuf {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 334,
            "text": "    let c = config::VmConfig::from_env();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 335,
            "text": "    session_gamma::workspace_parent_for_instance(\u0026c.lima_instance)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 336,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 337
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338,
            "text": "#[cfg(unix)]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 339,
            "text": "#[must_use]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "pub fn should_delegate_lima_shell("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 341,
            "text": "    vm_session: \u0026Rc\u003cRefCell\u003cSessionHolder\u003e\u003e,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 342,
            "text": "    is_tty: bool,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 343,
            "text": "    run_script: bool,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 344,
            "text": ") -\u003e bool {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 345,
            "text": "    if run_script || !is_tty {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 346,
            "text": "        return false;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 347,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 348,
            "text": "    if std::env::var(\"DEVSHELL_VM_INTERNAL_REPL\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 349,
            "text": "        .map(|s| {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 350,
            "text": "            let s = s.trim();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 351,
            "text": "            s == \"1\" || s.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"true\") || s.eq_ignore_ascii_case(\"yes\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 352,
            "text": "        })"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 353,
            "text": "        .unwrap_or(false)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 354,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 355,
            "text": "        return false;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 356,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 357,
            "text": "    matches!(*vm_session.borrow(), SessionHolder::Gamma(_))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 358,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 51,

            "text": "impl BetaSession {"

          },

            "text": "        }"

          {

          },
          {

          },
          {

            "text": "            .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())"

          {

          {

          },

          },
